title: platform-user — subprocessors
description: One optional subprocessor: PagerDuty for on-call rotation resolution + escalation paging. Optional — enabled per environment via `platform-user.on_call.enabled`. Off = the internal `on_call_rotation_entries` table backs OnCallResolver and no external calls fire. Even when enabled, only PlatformUser display_name + phone_e164 + department are sent to PagerDuty — never tenant data. Since PagerDuty processes ACADEMORIX employee data (not tenant end-user data), this is a workforce-management subprocessor arrangement, distinct from the tenant-side subprocessor tier that GDPR Art. 28 primarily governs.

subprocessors.name: PagerDuty
subprocessors.purpose: Backs the OnCallResolver when `platform-user.on_call.default_backend = pagerduty`. Owns the schedule authoring surface (Academorix ops maintains schedules in PagerDuty's own UI), the escalation-policy resolution, and the paging channels (SMS + phone + push). Academorix reads current-on-call via PagerDuty's REST API v2 (GET /oncalls filtered by escalation_policy_id).
data_processed: incident payloads at page time (short incident summary text; NEVER tenant data)
data_not_processed: any tenant-plane data — tenant PII never leaves the Academorix boundary via this integration
subprocessors.location: US-East (Amazon AWS us-east-1). PagerDuty is US-only for backend processing.
subprocessors.credential_management: PAGERDUTY_API_KEY resolved via Doppler; rotated quarterly. On rotation, the token is issued at PagerDuty then written to Doppler in the same maintenance window — no plaintext token ever lands on developer laptops.
subprocessors.failure_behaviour: OnCallResolverReachableProbe fails when PagerDuty is unreachable within 5s. Falls back to internal rotation table. `platform_user.on_call.resolver.miss.total{reason=backend_timeout|backend_error}` metric fires.

notes: PagerDuty is the ONE optional subprocessor. Off-by-default (`platform-user.on_call.enabled = false`) so a fresh environment does not silently create a PagerDuty account or leak data before the DPA is countersigned.
The `null` on-call backend is a no-op used in tests + when the resolver is disabled. Not a subprocessor.

Slack is NOT listed here — the internal-notifications module owns the Slack transport, and its subprocessor listing lives on that module's blueprint. PlatformUser dispatches TO Slack via that transport but does not independently engage Slack.

PII shared with PagerDuty is workforce-management PII (name + phone), not customer PII. This is a distinct GDPR data-processing category (Art. 6(1)(b) employment contract) from the tenant-plane subprocessor tier.
